clues.title: The Second Cipher Set
clues.description: A bundle of cipher notes identical in format to the Whitechapel originals, but with different symbols. These predate the murders by at least six months. Someone was testing the cipher system before deploying it.

clues.title: The First Victim
clues.description: The pawnshop owner remembers who brought in the cipher notes: a woman named Mary Kellner, who was found dead in Spitalfields three months before the Whitechapel murders began. Her death was ruled accidental — a fall from a window. But the cipher notes in her possession suggest otherwise.

clues.title: The Workshop
clues.description: Mary Kellner's former lodgings contain a hidden workshop — a small room behind a false wall, equipped with printing supplies and Grey Dawn reference materials. She was not a victim. She was the original cipher maker. Someone killed her and took over her operation.

clues.title: The Suspicious Landlord
clues.description: Kellner's landlord collected her belongings after her death and sold some to the pawnshop. He seems evasive, but his guilt is merely financial — he sold items that should have gone to her next of kin.
